coredata.dc:title: Crystal structures of T(T′, X)3 and T(T′, X)2 ternary compounds (T=Ti, Zr; T′=Au, Pt, Pd; X=Al, Ga)

coredata.dc:description: Ten new ternary titanium and zirconium based compounds have been synthesized by arc-melting of the elements. The crystal structure of the investigated intermetallics, determined by X-ray powder diffraction, has been related to the AuCu3 and MgCu2 structure types, respectively.

X-ray diffraction

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Rietveld profile refinement of X-ray diffraction data for the ternary compounds TiPd0.51Ga2.49 (a) and ZrAu0.54Ga2.46 (b).

Unit cell (a) and coordination polyhedra for the T (b) and X(T′) (c) atoms in the structure of the T(T′, X)3 (AuCu3 type) intermetallics. Dark circles indicate T (Ti or Zr) atoms and T′(X) (T′=Au, Pt, Pd; X=Al, Ga) atoms are marked by light circles.
Rietveld profile refinement of X-ray diffraction data for the ternary compounds ZrAu0.154Al1.846 (a) and ZrPt0.154Al1.846 (b).
Unit cell (a) and coordination polyhedra for the Zr (b) (dark circles) and Al(Au, Pt or Pd) (c) (light circles) atoms in the structure of the ternary compounds T(T′, X)2 (MgCu2 type).
Relations among the binary TX3 and the ternary T(T′, X)3 compounds, as well as T′ metal, where T=Ti, Zr; T′=Ag, Au, Pt, Pd; X=Al, Ga. Remark: (I) ZrAl3, (II) AuCu3, (III) TiAl3, (IV) Cu type.

(MgCu2 type) (T′=Ag, Au, Pt, Pd) (b) compounds.

article.ce:title: Crystal structures of T(T′, X)3 and T(T′, X)2 ternary compounds (T=Ti, Zr; T′=Au, Pt, Pd; X=Al, Ga)

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Inorganic Chemistry, Ivan Franko National University of Lviv, Kyryla and Mefodiya 6, 79005 Lviv, Ukraine

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Marian Smoluchowski Institute of Physics, Jagiellonian University, Reymonta 4, 30-059 Kraków, Poland

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Ten new ternary titanium and zirconium based compounds have been synthesized by arc-melting of the elements. The crystal structure of the investigated intermetallics, determined by X-ray powder diffraction, has been related to the AuCu3 and MgCu2 structure types, respectively.

ce:section.ce:para: Some of the titanium based alloys possess high mechanical durability, low thermal conductivity, a small factor of linear expansion, high corrosion stability or biocompatibility to living tissue. Zirconium and its alloys also possess valuable physical, chemical and mechanical properties, especially high radiation stability and low cross-section of capture of thermal neutrons. Aluminium and gallium alloys and intermetallics have wide practical applications. In order to develop new materials, there is an increasing interest for the investigation of the related alloys and intermetallic compounds [1].

Among 16 possible T–T′–X (T=Ti, Zr; T′=Ag, Au, Pt, Pd; X=Al, Ga) systems, the isothermal sections were investigated in the partial or whole concentration region only for the {Ti, Zr}–Ag–{Al, Ga} [2–6] and Ti–{Au, Pt, Pd}–Al [7,8] systems. Additionally, the existence of a certain number of isostructural ternary compounds of the discussed systems was found [9–15].
In this paper, we report the results of crystal structure investigations of new T(T′, X)3 and T(T′, X)2 intermetallic compounds found in the {Ti, Zr}–{Au, Pt, Pd}–{Al, Ga} systems.

Samples were prepared by arc-melting of initial components under high purity argon on a water-cooled copper hearth. Starting materials were used in the form of pieces of high purity metals (>99.9wt.%). The samples were re-melted three times for a better homogeneity. Ingots were afterwards sealed in evacuated quartz tubes and annealed at 600°C for two weeks. After heat treatment, the samples were quenched by submerging the silica tubes in cold water.

Phase analysis of the as-cast and annealed samples has been determined from the X-ray diffraction patterns obtained with Siemens D500 diffractometer (Cu Kα radiation). For the crystal structure determination of the ternary compounds, the following scanning parameters were used: 2θ region 15–140°, step scan 0.05°, counting time per step 10s. Theoretical diffractograms were calculated using the PowderCell programme [16]. The lattice parameters were obtained by least-squares fitting using the Latcon programme [17]. All the crystal structure calculations were performed by means of the Rietveld method using the FullProf programme [18].

In the as-cast samples and those annealed at 600°C (25at.% Ti or Zr) of the T–T′–X (T=Ti, Zr; T′=Au, Pt, Pd; X=Al, Ga) systems, we found new seven ternary compounds with AuCu3 structure type (space group
P m
3 ¯
m
). Atoms of T (Ti or Zr) occupy the positions of Au atoms, and while T′ (Au, Pt or Pd) and X (Al or Ga) atoms are statistically distributed over the positions of Cu in the AuCu3 structure. The results of the crystal structure determination for the T(T′, X)3 compounds are compiled in Table 1 . For example, results of the Rietveld profile refinement of the TiPd0.51Ga2.49 and ZrAu0.54Ga2.46 X-ray diffraction data are presented in Fig. 1 . The unit cell and coordination polyhedra for the atoms are given in Fig. 2 . Coordination polyhedra for the atoms of T and X(T′) are cubooctahedra with coordination number 12.

In the Zr–{Au, Pt, Pd}–Al systems on the cross-section ZrAl2–
Z ⁡
T ′
2
in annealed samples with small amount of the T′ element we found three Zr(T′, Al)2 (T′=Au, Pt or Pd) ternary compounds, corresponding to the MgCu2 structure type (space group
F d
3 ¯
m
). Here, atoms of Zr occupy the Mg site, and atoms of Al and Au, Pt or Pd are statistically distributed over the Cu site in this structure type. The results of the crystal structure determination for the Zr(T′, Al)2 compounds are collected in Table 2 . Results of the Rietveld profile refinement of the ZrT′ x Al3−x (T′=Au, Pt) X-ray diffraction data are presented in Fig. 3 . The unit cell and coordination polyhedra for the atoms are displayed in Fig. 4 . For the Zr atoms the coordination polyhedra are 16-vertex Frank-Kasper polyhedra (coordination number 16), and for the Al(Au, Pt or Pd) atoms are icosahedra (coordination number 12).

Based on the X-ray diffraction patterns of some as-cast and annealed samples (600°C) of the T–T′–X (T=Ti, Zr; T′=Au, Pt, Pd; X=Al, Ga) systems with concentration of 25 or 33.3at.% of the T metal, respectively, with a small amount of the T′ component, 10 intermetallics were found, which crystallize in the two known structure types, namely: AuCu3 and MgCu2. Interatomic distances for the T(T′, X)3 and T(T′, X)2 compounds are presented in Table 3 . These distances are close to the sum of the atomic radii of the respective atoms.

In the presently investigated and previously studied systems, a partial replacement of X atoms in binary compounds TX3 (T=Ti, Zr; T=Al, Ga) by T′ (Ag, Au, Pt or Pd) atoms leads to the homeotypic transformations of structures: TiAl3 →AuCu3 or ZrAl3 →AuCu3. Relationship among the structure types of the binary TX3 (ZrAl3 or TiAl3 types), the ternary T(T′, X)3 (AuCu3 type) compounds and the T′ metals with Cu type are shown in Fig. 5 .

Other homeotypic transitions of structures ZrAl2 (MgZn2 type)→
Zr ⁡
T ′
x
A
l
2 − x
(MgCu2 type) are observed on the cross-sections ZrAl2–
Zr ⁡
T ′
2
in the Zr–{Ag, Au, Pt, Pd}–Al systems. Both structural types of Laves phases are characterized by identical coordination numbers and coordination polyhedra of the atoms. They are polytypes with different sequences of the layers (Fig. 6 ).

To conclude, in the T–T′–X (T=Ti, Zr; T′=Ag, Au, Pt, Pd; X=Al, Ga) systems 18 ternary compounds with AuCu3 and MgCu2 structure types were found until now. The regularities of the crystal structures of the ternary compounds in the investigated and related ternary systems are presented and discussed.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by the International Visegrad Fund (IVF) (Serial Number: US-018-2005).

ce:other-ref.ce:textref: I.L. Knunyants, Chemical Encyclopedia, vols. 1–5, Moscow, 1988–1998 (in Russian).

sb:title.sb:maintitle: IX International Conference on Crystal Chemistry of Intermetallic Compounds
